Single visit based approaches

Analysing Ecological Data with Detection Error

Author

Péter Sólymos

Published

April 21, 2026

Preamble

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    library(unmarked)
    library(detect)
    library(dplyr)
    library(ggplot2)
    theme_set(theme_light())
})
set.seed(1234)

Field data analysis

We will use the ABMI data, which has 3 equal (3.3 min) time intervals for each point count visit. We use the point level data, but only for the center point, so we can keep sample size small and run the code quickly. When analyzing such data sets for real, we would use all the data and not subset it like this.

if (!interactive()) {
    d <- readRDS("../data/ab_birds_site_year_pt.rds")
} else {
    d <- readRDS("data/ab_birds_site_year_pt.rds")
}

spp <- "yellowrumped_warbler"

x <- data.frame(
    y1 = ifelse(d$visits$visit1[, spp] > 0, 1, 0),
    y2 = ifelse(d$visits$visit2[, spp] > 0, 1, 0),
    y3 = ifelse(d$visits$visit3[, spp] > 0, 1, 0),
    n1 = d$visits$visit1[, spp],
    n2 = d$visits$visit2[, spp],
    n3 = d$visits$visit3[, spp],
    d$vars
)
x$yday <- as.POSIXlt(x$fielddate)$yday / 365
x$hr <- x$fieldtime / 24
x$y_max <- pmax(x$y1, x$y2, x$y3)
x$n_max <- pmax(x$n1, x$n2, x$n3)
x <- x |>
    filter(
        hr > 0.1,
        hr < 0.6,
        yday > 0.37,
        yday < 0.5
    )
x0 <- x[x$point == 1, ]

Single visit occupancy

m_sv <- svocc(
    y1 ~ pconif + I(pconif^2) + thf | hr + I(hr^2) + yday,
    data = x0,
    link.sta = "logit"
)
summary(m_sv)

Call:
svocc(formula = y1 ~ pconif + I(pconif^2) + thf | hr + I(hr^2) + yday, 
    data = x0, link.sta = "logit")


Single visit site-occupancy model
Maximum Likelihood estimates (optim method)

Occupancy model coefficients with logit link:
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)  -0.1023     0.3111  -0.329   0.7423    
pconif        8.5911     4.9674   1.729   0.0837 .  
I(pconif^2)  -4.4403     5.3427  -0.831   0.4059    
thf          -3.3370     0.6002  -5.560  2.7e-08 ***
Detection model coefficients with logit link:
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
(Intercept)    2.729      2.682   1.017   0.3089  
hr             7.566     10.785   0.702   0.4830  
I(hr^2)       -8.092     17.133  -0.472   0.6367  
yday          -9.511      5.031  -1.890   0.0587 .
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 

Log-likelihood: -369.9 on 8 Df
AIC = 755.9 
fit_sv2 <- plogis(
    model.matrix(formula(m_sv)$sta, x0) %*% coef(m_sv, model = "sta")
)
summary(fit_sv2)
       V1         
 Min.   :0.03109  
 1st Qu.:0.05752  
 Median :0.47445  
 Mean   :0.49834  
 3rd Qu.:0.94831  
 Max.   :0.98293  
data.frame(x0, p_occ = fit_sv2) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = pconif, y = p_occ, col = thf)) +
    geom_point() +
    ylim(0, 1)
d2 <- plogis(
    model.matrix(formula(m_sv)$det, x0) %*% coef(m_sv, model = "det")
)
summary(d2)
       V1        
 Min.   :0.3286  
 1st Qu.:0.4400  
 Median :0.5148  
 Mean   :0.5039  
 3rd Qu.:0.5515  
 Max.   :0.6712  
data.frame(x0, p_det = d2) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = hr, y = p_det, col = yday)) +
    geom_point() +
    ylim(0, 1)
data.frame(x0, p_det = d2) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = yday, y = p_det, col = hr)) +
    geom_point() +
    ylim(0, 1)
# sometimes it is hard to get SEs, use bootstrap
b <- bootstrap(m_sv, B = 250)
Warning in svocc.fit(Y, X, Z, link.sta, link.det, penalized = penalized, :
negative variance values in optim, NAs produced
summary(b, type = "boot")

Call:
svocc(formula = y1 ~ pconif + I(pconif^2) + thf | hr + I(hr^2) + yday, 
    data = x0, link.sta = "logit")


Single visit site-occupancy model
Maximum Likelihood estimates (optim method)
with Nonparametric Bootstrap standard errors (B = 250)

Occupancy model coefficients with logit link:
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)  -0.1023     0.4509  -0.227    0.821    
pconif        8.5911    33.3887   0.257    0.797    
I(pconif^2)  -4.4403    26.7084  -0.166    0.868    
thf          -3.3370     0.6379  -5.231 1.69e-07 ***
Detection model coefficients with logit link:
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
(Intercept)    2.729     11.419   0.239    0.811
hr             7.566     36.451   0.208    0.836
I(hr^2)       -8.092     27.812  -0.291    0.771
yday          -9.511     18.751  -0.507    0.612
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 

Log-likelihood: -369.9 on 8 Df
AIC = 755.9 
# explain parallelization options for bootstrap

Compare multiple and single visit results:

umf2 <- unmarked::unmarkedFrameOccu(
    y = x0[, c("y1", "y2", "y3")],
    siteCovs = x0[, c("pconif", "thf")],
    obsCovs = list(
        hr = x0[, c("hr", "hr", "hr")],
        yday = x0[, c("yday", "yday", "yday")]
    )
)
m_mv2 <- unmarked::occu(
    ~ hr + I(hr^2) + yday ~ pconif + I(pconif^2) + thf,
    umf2
)
fit_mv2 <- plogis(model.matrix(m_mv2@formula[-2], x0) %*% coef(m_mv2, type = "state"))

data.frame(p_occ = fit_mv2, p_max = fit_sv2) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = fit_mv2, y = fit_sv2)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_abline(lty = 2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
mean(fit_mv2 / fit_sv2)
[1] 0.7744198

Single visit N-mixture

m_sv <- svabu(
    n1 ~ pconif + I(pconif^2) + thf | hr + yday,
    data = x0,
    N.max = 100,
    zeroinfl = FALSE
)
summary(m_sv)

Call:
svabu(formula = n1 ~ pconif + I(pconif^2) + thf | hr + yday, data = x0, 
    zeroinfl = FALSE, N.max = 100)

Single visit Binomial - Poisson model
Conditional Maximum Likelihood estimates

Coefficients for abundance (log link):
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept)  -0.8137     0.4631  -1.757 0.078930 .  
pconif        3.1805     0.6198   5.132 2.87e-07 ***
I(pconif^2)  -2.1405     0.5992  -3.572 0.000354 ***
thf          -1.7918     0.2836  -6.319 2.63e-10 ***
Coefficients for detection (logit link):
            Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
(Intercept)    8.665      7.847   1.104    0.269
hr             4.361      5.751   0.758    0.448
yday         -21.279     17.872  -1.191    0.234
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 

Log-likelihood: -579.3 on 7 Df
AIC =  1173 
fit_mv <- exp(
    model.matrix(formula(m_sv)$sta, x0) %*% coef(m_sv, model = "sta")
)
summary(fit_mv)
       V1         
 Min.   :0.07386  
 1st Qu.:0.10420  
 Median :0.43309  
 Mean   :0.57000  
 3rd Qu.:1.11729  
 Max.   :1.44443  
data.frame(x0, lam = fit_mv) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = pconif, y = lam, col = thf)) +
    geom_point()
d2 <- plogis(
    model.matrix(formula(m_sv)$det, x0) %*% coef(m_sv, model = "det")
)
summary(d2)
       V1        
 Min.   :0.3364  
 1st Qu.:0.5366  
 Median :0.6858  
 Mean   :0.6506  
 3rd Qu.:0.7436  
 Max.   :0.9037  
data.frame(x0, p_det = d2) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = hr, y = p_det, col = yday)) +
    geom_point()
data.frame(x0, p_det = d2) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = yday, y = p_det, col = hr)) +
    geom_point()

Exercise: Try using svabu() with the 2nd and 3rd visits.

What if we stack visits and assume independence?

x1 <- x0 |>
    dplyr::select(n1, n2, n3, pconif, thf, hr, yday) |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
        cols = starts_with("n"),
        names_to = "visit",
        values_to = "n"
    )
m_sv123 <- svabu(
    n ~ pconif + I(pconif^2) + thf | hr + yday,
    data = x1,
    N.max = 100,
    zeroinfl = FALSE
)
summary(m_sv123)

Model selection is more complicated for single visit models, because we need to make sure the model form satisfies the RSPF condition.

The following strategy is recommended for single visit models:

  1. Fit the most complex model (saturated) for both the status and detection components
  2. Then simplify the model by removing detection covariates one by one, starting with the least significant ones, and checking the AIC at each step
  3. Make sure that detection probability has sufficient variation (e.g. by plotting it against covariates), i.e. it is not close to constant, otherwise the model may not be identifiable and estimates may be unreliable
  4. Once you have a reasonable detection model, you can simplify the status component by removing covariates one by one, starting with the least significant ones, and checking the AIC at each step
m_sv_full <- svabu(
    n1 ~ pconif + I(pconif^2) + thf | hr + I(hr^2) + yday,
    data = x0,
    N.max = 100,
    zeroinfl = FALSE
)
summary(m_sv_full)

drop1(m_sv_full, model = "det") # no huge drop in AIC
m_sv_red <- update(m_sv_full, formula = . ~ . | . - I(hr^2))
summary(m_sv_red)
drop1(m_sv_red, model = "sta") # no better model

Exercise: Try zeroinfl = TRUE and distr = "NB" to explore other model forms. Compare the models using AIC.

m_sv_P <- m_sv_red
m_sv_ZIP <- update(m_sv_P, zeroinfl = TRUE)
m_sv_NB <- update(m_sv_P, distr = "NB")
m_sv_ZINB <- update(m_sv_P, distr = "NB", zeroinfl = TRUE)

AIC(m_sv_P, m_sv_NB, m_sv_ZIP, m_sv_ZINB)
summary(m_sv_ZIP)

RSPF condition

RSPF condition reduces to the following two conditions:

  1. not all covariates in the model are categorical, and
  2. the ratio function is nonlinear and involves all components of the parameter vector

Remember that if the nonlinearity on the log-scale is weak, one may need very large sample sizes to get reasonable estimates.

Use the shiny app to explore issues when both the status and detection components of the model are constants:

shiny::runApp("apps/mvisit")
